title: Mouse Pointer Icon
text: Mouse Pointer Icon Type of image to use as a background or icon— Glyph or File . Value Description (None) No image is used Glyph Use the specified glyph. File Use the specified file. Description This property defines the type of image to use as the drag and drop icon. Specify the image to use in the Glyph or Image File property, as appropriate. If you specify a Glyph , it is useful to use glyphs that have both primary and secondary images. If you use a glyph that has no secondary image, the cursor will be square and non-transparent. If you want the cursor to be non-rectangular (for example an arrow), you should define a monochrome secondary glyph, in which white pixels replace those where you want the cursor to be transparent. Applies to Drag-and-Drop Formats Related Links Glyphs Glyphs are images in a Uniface-proprietary format, which can be used as background images in forms, entities and application shells, and as icons in widgets, panel buttons, menus, and drag-and-drop formats.
